evals.name: check-before-naming
evals.prompt: I want to add a new function called escape_like to the glossary project at g:/python_projects/skills_creator/glossary. It will handle escaping SQL patterns. Before I start, check if there's anything with a similar name already.
evals.expected_output: Should find the existing escape_like function in scripts/glossary_common.py and warn about the naming conflict

assertions.name: found-existing-escape_like
assertions.check: The output mentions that escape_like already exists

assertions.name: identified-file-location
assertions.check: The output identifies glossary_common.py as the file containing escape_like

assertions.name: suggested-alternative
assertions.check: The output suggests an alternative name or warns about the conflict

assertions.name: token-efficiency
assertions.check: Used glossary_search instead of reading source files

evals.name: session-start-orientation
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new session working on the glossary skill at g:/python_projects/skills_creator/glossary. What symbols exist in this project? Show me all functions and classes across the codebase so I can orient myself.
evals.expected_output: Should provide a structured overview of glossary symbols organized by module without reading every source file

assertions.name: covered-server
assertions.check: The output lists glossary_server.py symbols (glossary_search, glossary_init, etc.)

assertions.name: covered-common
assertions.check: The output mentions glossary_common.py symbols (find_project_root, escape_like, etc.)

assertions.name: covered-scanner
assertions.check: The output mentions glossary_scanner.py

assertions.name: structured-output
assertions.check: Output is organized by module/file, not a random dump

evals.name: find-duplicates
evals.prompt: Check the glossary project at g:/python_projects/skills_creator/glossary for duplicate symbol names — any functions or classes that share the same name across different files.
evals.expected_output: Should use glossary_duplicates tool and report any symbols with the same name in multiple files

assertions.name: used-duplicates-tool
assertions.check: Used glossary_duplicates MCP tool (not manual file reading)

assertions.name: reported-results
assertions.check: The output either lists specific duplicates or confirms no duplicates found

assertions.name: actionable-output
assertions.check: If duplicates found, explains which files and suggests whether they're intentional

evals.name: post-compaction-recovery
evals.prompt: My context was just compacted and I lost track of the glossary project at g:/python_projects/skills_creator/glossary. I was working on the MCP server — what tools does it expose and what helper functions does it use?
evals.expected_output: Should query glossary to restore knowledge of server tools and helpers without reading full source

assertions.name: found-mcp-tools
assertions.check: Lists MCP tool functions: glossary_search, glossary_file, glossary_type, etc.

assertions.name: found-helpers
assertions.check: Mentions helper functions: _get_db, _get_lock, _handle_error

assertions.name: token-efficiency
assertions.check: Used glossary tools to restore knowledge, not full file reads

evals.name: partial-path-file-lookup
evals.prompt: Show me all the symbols in the common module of the glossary project at g:/python_projects/skills_creator/glossary. I think the file is called something like common.py.
evals.expected_output: Should use glossary_file with partial path 'common.py' and show all symbols in glossary_common.py

assertions.name: matched-partial-path
assertions.check: Found and displayed symbols from glossary_common.py despite partial path input

assertions.name: showed-key-symbols
assertions.check: Lists find_project_root, escape_like, format_symbol, group_by_file

assertions.name: used-glossary-file
assertions.check: Used glossary_file tool, not source file reading

evals.name: negative-should-not-trigger
evals.prompt: Explain how the glossary_search function works internally — I want to understand the SQL query it builds and how wildcard patterns are converted to LIKE syntax. The project is at g:/python_projects/skills_creator/glossary.
evals.expected_output: Should read the actual source code of glossary_search, not just query the glossary. The user wants implementation details, not a symbol listing.

assertions.name: read-source-code
assertions.check: Read or referenced actual source code of glossary_search function body, not just symbol listing

assertions.name: explained-sql-logic
assertions.check: Explains the LIKE pattern conversion, escape_like usage, or SQL query construction

assertions.name: not-just-glossary-dump
assertions.check: Did NOT rely solely on glossary tools — actually read implementation details
